analysis:
Tested DINOv2 feature extraction at an increased resolution of 448x448 (from 336x336). The extracted 1024 patches were average-pooled and concatenated with the CLS token (still a 3072-D representation) and trained with the standard Normalized Focal Loss.
Result: 0.6786.
Conclusion: Scaling up the native spatial resolution massively boosts the baseline focal loss model (0.6740 -> 0.6786). This is the best individual improvement found, beating the 336x336 Normalized ASL model (0.6773). High-res patch semantics explicitly help multi-label classification.
